scenario_name: Scenario 20 — Expert Review: Separating Signal from Noise
justification: While Kobe correctly identifies the uncapped refund amount as a Critical finding (#2), the review fails on signal-to-noise discipline. There are 3 Critical findings when only 1 is genuinely critical. The idempotency issue (#1) and atomicity issue (#3) are invented critical-level concerns — they are legitimate general engineering concerns but are not present as critical bugs in the code under review (the scenario tests whether Kobe can distinguish the ONE real critical issue from plausible-sounding but contextually inappropriate findings). Additionally, there are 2 Important issues and 3 suggestions, totaling 8 findings when the rubric expects at most 2-3 total. The log injection finding is a false positive — console.log with orderId is appropriate for an internal service. The review is padded with findings to appear thorough, which is exactly the failure mode the scenario tests against.

observations.text: Correctly identifies uncapped refund amount as Critical with good reproduction steps and fix
Idempotency elevated to Critical is an invented critical finding — this is a general distributed systems concern, not a bug visible in the code's acceptance criteria
Atomicity between payment and DB write elevated to Critical is another invented finding — reasonable engineering concern but not the signal this scenario tests for
3 Critical findings when rubric expects exactly 1 genuinely critical finding — demonstrates poor signal-to-noise ratio
Log injection flagged as Important is a false positive for an internal service context
8 total findings (3 critical + 2 important + 3 suggestions) when rubric expects 1 critical and at most 1-2 non-critical — classic padding behavior
Review fails to demonstrate the restraint and signal-to-noise discipline the scenario specifically tests for
